publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 80-81. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest William. I am a little vexed with you for not attending to my particular request that you would return me Dr Locock's letter, which I wanted this morning to show to Dr Ferguson, who came to make acquaintance with me, as I did not think it at all desirable to defer the awkwardness of the first interview
& explanations until I am actually ill.-- I like him very much, and he has placed the contingent attendance on a most liberal footing.--
He thinks I shall be very probably not above an hour or two ill.-- He considers that it is very possible I may remain till after the 24th, which would be certainly still the most agreeable to me.--
I have had some conversation with Hester today, which has interested me very much, though it has also caused

me some pain. I will not give the details, which would be long, until you return, but I have at last with much difficulty got at the cause of the reluctance to come to Ockham, or at least at one very principal cause.--

She has not refused to remain on here, & it is left doubtful, but at all events she is not going just yet, & seems to consider it nearly fixed that she should stay for a week or two after my confinement takes place, unless the event should be

Further circumstance I have clearly ascertained, & am going to write to Woburn about it, viz: that Hester intends going with your mother, & Charlotte is to remain behind. This seems pretty well settled I think, but I fear there will be a difficulty about getting Charlotte to Ockham during their absence.-- However, I do not despair, especially as I think you have it much in power to remove their objections, as I will explain when I see you.--
